channel.title: Fraser's Newsletter
channel.description: Welcome to my newsletter! I started making this website at the start of 2025 and I've enjoyed growing it to where it is today. I get that it's hard to constantly check the site for updates, so to make things easier for you guys, I've prepared a newsletter which features all the new content from the website whenever I update it. If you're struggling to read the RSS feed it's fine, I get it. You can just read it directly on my website! https://worldpeace.services/newsletter

item.title: Landlords
item.description:
Hello and welcome to another newsletter, this time it's landlord edition. I've been struggling to get my head around how some people in a place like Germany have enough money to buy property, and yes, even though you spend your whole life paying off a mortgage, you own that property and can rent it out, or live there and pay no rent yourself.
If you're one of those lucky people, and you choose to rent out your flat, then someone is paying you hundreds of euros a month, simply because you own the thing. You're not even providing a service to that person, it's just because you own it and they don't. Sure, rents these days consist of many things, and lots of Nebenkosten are involved, but ultimately, the Kaltmiete that is being transferred is only happening because someone else got to that point where they could buy the flat.
I reckon most homeowners are just privileged motherfuckers who aren't inherently better than renters. I reckon we've reached such a point in capitalism in western Europe that this is mainly old money that is buying property. It's not the case that people who work the hardest get to own property. Also, once you pay off your mortgage, then suddenly you have passive income and can just work less! It doesn't seem fair. And that's because it isn't, because capitalism.
Anyway, I've decided to become a landlord. The last 8 years of working as a software developer as well as living pretty frugally has led to enough Eigenkapital to put down a down payment for a 2 room flat. I've decided to do it as a 'Kapitalanlage', somewhere safe to put my money, and hopefully the value of the property will appreciate and resist the inevitable inflation nonsense that will hit when various world crises happen. It was either this or speculate on stocks and shares, something which I'm fundamentally struggling with. Like, do I have do that?
A friend of mine is moving into the flat, and I'll stay in my cheap rental flat. I think it's best money-wise this way, and it means I don't have to leave my lovely flat. All I have to do is concentrate really hard and not be a dickhead landlord. It turns out it's really easy to do that: you just follow the rules and do Mietspiegel price and give an unlimited contract. I will start receiving passive income from the tenant, but I will also be paying off the mortgage for like 20 years so I'm not going to feel like a true capitalist for a while, I don't think.
In other news, I added a fun feature to the website's homepage. You can upload an image there, and it will display in my house! I attached a tiny monitor to the raspberry pi that this website is running on. I think it could lead to some fun shenanigans. All you need is my wifi password (you can just ask me for that).

Game Review

Minecraft Dungeons

Vibes: 2, Gameplay: 6, Platform: Playstation 5, Release: 2020

I enjoyed getting super stoned and playing this with Anni and May, although I quickly realised they were a lot better at it that me. Turns out this game has a roguelike mode! On our second attempt we almost made it to the top of the tower which is pretty good going.
I got the hang of it, but it's honestly not better than for example a Hades. And the general vibes of the game isn't there if you ask me. The music is positively terrible. I'm told there's much more in the game outside of the roguelite mode to explore and maybe that's a bit better.

Film Review

The Invisible Man

Release: 1933, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

An old film about a man who turns himself invisible. Super entertaining. Lots to enjoy here. I'm loving classic films like this.

Film Review

Nightbreed

Release: 1990, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

A very sexually charged and homoerotic horror action film where the money is invested more into the costumes and sets than the writing. Really funny.

Film Review

Brazil

Release: 1985

A dystopian 1984-type film which had some great characters and funny moments, but I honestly got a bit bored and started playing pokemon leaf green during the last hour or so.

Film Review

What Ever Happened To Baby Jane?

Release: 1962

A camp black and white film about a sour old lady who terrorises everyone around her. It's very entertaining and honestly quite shocking.

Film Review

Pink Narcissus

Release: 1971, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

A vibes-heavy 70's film about homoerotic imagery and sex fantasies without any dialogue. It's got great colours but I was really tired in the cinema and just wanted to go home.

Album Review

Vendrán Suaves Lluvias

Artist: Silvana Estrada, Release: 2025, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the albums page!

Gorgeous new release from Silvana. She continues to stun with her amazing voice. This one has a higher production value than previous albums, especially when it comes to the strings. Love the strings specifically in the song 'Flores', and the final song 'El Alma Mia' is just plain gorgeous. Something about ending an albums with humming like that, even though the song is so upsetting in topic.

Album Review

Superbloom

Artist: Jessie Ware, Release: 2026, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the albums page!

Well guys she did it again! Jessie is back for round 3 of heavenly disco-infused pop magic. She's taken the breezy string arrangements from 'Remember Where You Are' and 'Hey Love' and turned them into a whole album, and because of that it sounds like angelic heaven music. It feels like a warm breeze. When I first heard 'Ride' I thought it was cheesy but after like 3 listens I became a fan. I just love Ennio Morricone that much. Also, shoutouts to my favourite track 'Don't You Know Who I Am?'. I can just pictured the woman scorned on the dancefloor now her planned hook up failed. Iconic song.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Pikmin

Platform: Gamecube, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

There are no words to describe my love for the Pikmin soundtrack. The original Pikmin is where it all began. All this idiosyncratic, loopy-doopy music somehow making it into a mainline Nintendo game is just wild. It's honestly all over the place and I love it. Just listen to 'The Impact Site' which exemplifies the series pretty well, I think. Although I will say the decision to include not just the normal area themes but also the battle and sunset variants (and sunset + battle) in the tracks, making them 6+ minutes does kinda ruin the listening experience a bit. That being said, some of the sunset music is genuinely great (like Forest Navel).
Favourites in this soundtrack include the beautiful and peaceful 'Forest of Hope', the stunning 'Distant Spring' (although the battle version ruins the track a bit), and the magical 'Today's Report'. I remember playing that on the piano constantly. I think back that it open my eyes to this particular chord sequence.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

The Legend of Zelda: A Link Between Worlds

Platform: 3DS, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

Because this leans so heavily on a Link to the Past's soundtrack, I feel like it doesn't have too much of its own identity. I do think it's good though. Specifically the 'Three Dungeons of Hyrule' theme is so moody and excellent. And the brand new 'Lorule Castle' is one of the all-time greats.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Yoshi

Platform: Gameboy, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

It's not very good. Don't spend any time on this.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Yoshi

Platform: NES, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

I don't think there's much to enjoy here. 'Vs. Menu' is positively horrible.

Concert

Silvana Estrada

Date: 10-05-2026, Venue: Heimathafen, Berlin

Deeply emotional performance from Silvana. Great to see her now three times in Berlin, each time she's bigger and better. For the final song we all sang 'El Alma Mia' together without microphones and I loved it. Great venue, also.

Fun video for your entertainment

At last, I am free

Link: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GzgavGowD_A

This one gets me pissing myself with laughter every time.

Fun video for your entertainment

Name a yellow fruit

Link: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SBeYzoQPbu8

We've all been there.

New CD

Inferno

Artist: Boards of Canada

Got this at a release party that I went to at Rough Trade with Leo.

New CD

Vendrán Suaves Lluvias

Artist: Silvana Estrada

Peaceful, gorgeous music from Mexico. You can read about it in the albums section.

New CD

Marchita

Artist: Silvana Estrada

Peaceful, beautiful music from Mexico. You can read about it in the albums section.

And don't forget... an archive of "Soundtrack of the Day" updates are on the website!

item.title: Making my own music and video library
item.description:
Recently I had a super stressful time in my life where I didn't make time for me, so I took the week off work! Not only did I get all the shit on my to-do list done, but I also finally had some time for some Fraser projects. This time that meant taking some steps on my journey of de-teching my life. I made my own music and movie streaming platform on the raspberry pi in my house!
I already had the pi set up with cloudflare and nginx for this website, so it was pretty easy to install some open source music streaming software (navidrome) and video streaming software (jellyfin). I connected two SSDs to the pi and made a nice network-attached storage out it! That part was a learning experience. I've had to learn about voltage and self-hosting stuff and let's just say I'm happy it works now.
Movie streaming is available at watch.worldpeace.services. I downloaded a bunch of films that I've been meaning to watch and now no matter where I am, I can log in and stream them! If any readers of this website want in, let me know and I'll make you a login.
Likewise, music streaming is available at music.worldpeace.services, but here I'm at a crossroads in terms of how I set up my library. I started by putting all my CDs on there, but it doesn't feel right illegally downloading the music, when one of the reasons I want to stop using other streaming services is because they barely pay the artists. On the other hand, I'm daunted by the idea of buying all my favourite albums. There are a lot.
Also, I'm still going to need a streaming service so I can listen to new music before I commit to downloading it and putting on the pi. So honestly, I'm not sure what to do about music streaming. I can build up a banger library over time, but it's not a satisfying solution. Anyway if you want to listen to the CDs I have at home, let me know and I'll give you a login here too.
Anyway, I wish you guys well if you're also on a de-teching journey, and I wish you all a lovely time! Let's go into the summer with free calendars!

Game Review

Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream

Vibes: 10, Gameplay: 4, Platform: Switch 2, Release: 2026

This game is not worth the price because the gameplay is interesting for 10 minutes at a time. But I have to give it to this series for being genuinely funny. It's so absurd and the comedic timing is just a little off, and I love it. It's so funny. And it's only possible thanks to the ability to make the Miis say whatever you want. My Mii's favourite hobby? Cock and ball torture. Favourite food? Pile of steaming shit. What do the Miis bond over most? Domming.
I was showing this game to Jack and Anni when two Miis had a baby and gave it a video of porn (that I drew) as its first childhood toy. Out of context it was actually outrageous and I understand why Nintendo don't let you upload screenshots from this game, because there are absolutely no limits or filters on the user input. Super funny.
Anyway, the game itself really doesn't have much going for it. It gets boring when you start seeing the same cutscenes over and over again.

Film Review

Scherbenland

Release: 2026

An interesting glimpse of punk Berlin! It focused on a 70s punk band (great), a female singer-songwriter (great) and a group of three local lads who are doing Deutschrap (not great). The lads were cute because they are best mates, but they can't answer interview questions for shit and aren't exactly carrying the punk politics torch.

Film Review

The Drama

Release: 2026, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

An absolutely excruciating film in terms of second-hand embarrassment. All these rich Americans are deplorable.

Film Review

Only Good Things

Release: 2025

Watched this at mongay. It has a vibes heavy first half that I really enjoyed, but I wasn't really on board with the tone shift in the second half.

Album Review

Crazymad, For Me

Artist: CMAT, Release: 2023, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the albums page!

I keep coming back to this album, mainly thanks to the huge banger that is 'Stay For Something'. I love that she made the climax of the song admitting that she can't bring herself to hate her ex. I'm also obsessed with the special vibe of 'I hate who I am when I'm horny', which explains her complicated feelings about failed experiences. That's a theme of CMAT's music, making songs which showcases the wide range of strange emotions she is feeling.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Starfox

Platform: Switch 2, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

Hard to believe they're remaking Lylat Wars for the fourth time but here we are. At least that game had some great tracks on the soundtrack! Here we have some bombastic, orchestral versions of the old tracks. I'm not sure if it does anything for me. Starfox Zero had some real great music so maybe they can pull it out the bag.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Mario Tennis Aces

Platform: Switch, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

This really is a pile of steaming shit and not worth your attention. The Bowser's Road remix is alright I guess.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream

Platform: Switch, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

If there's one thing that Nintendo love putting on this app, it's every single Mii-themed game. So you just know that the new Tomodachi Life is coming on here frame 1. I'm not wowed by the music so far if I'm honest. The previous game had some good deep cuts but these tracks here aren't doing it for me.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Animal Crossing

Platform: Gamecube, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

This soundtrack is very kooky indeed. It makes me appreciate how Animal Crossing is a series which took a fair few games of perfecting the formula. This is pretty rough, but almost all of this is also in New Horizons because its foundations are so solid. The hourly themes vary quite a lot, some resembling newer hourly themes, and others way more out there. Some of them (looking at you, 11pm and 3pm) are borderline unlistenable.
Also, I want to shout out this beautiful little chord sequence buried deep in the new year tracks. Specifically the 10 minutes before midnight music is super gorgeous. And 'Meteor Shower' and 'Wishing Well' are beautiful and never came back in future iterations!

New Photo in the Gallery

Biggest 1. Mai yet

For anecdotes about the pictures, please visit the gallery page!

None

New Photo in the Gallery

Crew Battle for the ages

For anecdotes about the pictures, please visit the gallery page!

None

New Photo in the Gallery

Bowling for success

For anecdotes about the pictures, please visit the gallery page!

None

Fun video for your entertainment

Little smirk

Link: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/lMjCvQB7uyY

It's all about that first tiny smirk. Obsessed.

Fun video for your entertainment

Nobody

Link: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/wkeCoNhTgp0

Love everything about this, especially the grand exit at the end.

And don't forget... an archive of "Soundtrack of the Day" updates are on the website!

item.title: A different kind of party world
item.description:
Recently I was scammed out of 400€, partly because of my own negligence, and partly because prepaidmobilerecharge.com is a fucking scam. About a year ago I got a grip on my finances and made a spreadsheet with all my monthly payments, and it seemed fine. About a month later I topped up 15€ on a prepaid mobile sim card. I did it in a hurry because I needed it very quick, and the normal Telekom service was taking ages. The money never even arrived in the end but whatever, eventually the normal service kicked in and I forgot about it.
Turns out, this third-party website I used secretly signed me up for a 50€ monthly subscription. I didn't check my bank's outgoing payments for many months and before I knew it, I lost a bunch of money. I managed to get some back, but I wasn't seeing that 400€ again unless I went to court or something. So dear readers, my advice to you is to turn on notifications from your bank app so you have a good overview of what money goes in and out of your account.
Anyway, around this time, my friend Ort asked me if I wanted to DJ at a disco party that his new company is putting on, and I decided to give it a go because a) it would be a fun experience being a professional DJ, b) always good to try new things and c) his company would pay me 400€ for it. Bam! I can solve this problem with the power of disco music. Perfect.
The party happened yesterday and it really was a fascinating look a different world. It went from 6pm until 9pm and was marketed as 'Gimme Gimme Disco - an ABBA-inspired Disco party'. It was marketed at like 50+ year olds and that was indeed the crowd. They were all there on the dot, mostly older women, either in groups of girlfriends or dragging their husbands along, and to be fair they all had great sparkly outfits. They also had the best time ever. I had a very easy time on the DJ decks pleasing them because I just had to play pop songs and they would go crazy. There was even a scripted moment where there were balloons when Dancing Queen came on half way through.
The whole thing was giving Kleinstadt-Deutschland. I wouldn't be surprised if they all went home to Brandenburg after the event. These are the kinds of events you see advertised on posters around town, and they sell out! It was 20€ a pop for the ticket so fair enough to Ort's company for making good buck from it. I think past me would have judged someone for going to a 6pm ABBA party but now I realise that they were happy and who am I to judge them. I was the DJ and I'm not about to judge myself for it.
Anyway that is this week's story time, I hope you enjoyed it. On the website I redesigned the 'Misc' page to be even less usable. Maybe the stuff on there is not meant to be actually clicked on, yknow. I've also been watching lots of films. As I write this I am still emotionally recovering from the most devastating film ever, Sirat. Honestly, I can't recommend it because it's that nightmarish. Go check your finances instead. See you next time.

Game Review

Promise Mascot Agency

Vibes: 8, Gameplay: 6, Platform: Switch 2, Release: 2025

I was a huge fan of the vibe-heavy Paradise Killer, and the devs of that made a new game so I gave it a go, but the music isn't as good. I didn't realise before buying that they have a new composer and yeah it's just not the same. Also, the vibe of the game is quite düster and grey/brown visually, and I don't love it.
The premise of the game is fucking wild. Whoever came up with this is insane. It's about a yakuza who owes millions and is banished to a cursed town to start a 'Mascot Agency'. In Japan there is a big culture of mascots, people wearing silly outfits to promote events, and in this game mascots are real, horrible monsters. You have to run the business with a massive finger called Pinky☆.
I don't think I'll complete this game. In the end it's a management sim and when the mascots need help on their events you have to do this little minigame which always has the same music and quickly gets boring. It constantly interrupts the normal gameplay, which is driving around in an indestructible truck. It's actually not a chill game at all.

Game Review

Slay the Spire 2

Vibes: 7, Gameplay: 11, Platform: PC, Release: 2026

Oh god here we go again. The first Slay the Spire kept me going for years, and I finally managed to spend the last year in peace having beaten A20 heart with all the characters. Now we've been served a whole-ass sequel with twice as much content. It's so over! I'll see you guys on the other side!!
I'm so excited to play this game in Early Access! The first time I've done that. I'm going to be there for each and every patch and it's going to be delicious. I've already identified the most broken cards and I look forward to the nerfs!
Also, I love the multiplayer! It works so good. So nice to bond with friends like this.

Film Review

Sirat

Release: 2025, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

I will never forget this film. Watching it feels like a nightmare.

Film Review

A World Not Ours

Release: 2012

A film about Ein el-Helweh, a refugee camp in Lebanon filled with Palestinians. Fascinating. For me it was a reminder how fucked it is that refugees can't work. In this camp they are trapped there and they don't do any proper work because they don't have the papers. All over the world it's the same story, asylum seekers twiddle their thumbs in places like the UK when they could be working, and they want to work. It's fucked.

Film Review

Mar Mama

Release: 2023

Upsetting short film about settler violence in the West Bank. Things like this are happening right now in Palestine. These horrible settlers are horrible and fuck the Israeli security in the region who support the settlers.

Film Review

Vibrations from Gaza

Release: 2024

Upsetting short film about deaf kids in Gaza pre-October 7. There's a school of deaf kids there and I was just asking myself, has it been bombed now?

Film Review

The History of Sound

Release: 2025, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

I was setting myself to not enjoy this because *rolls eyes* the power of music. But actually I got totally swept away by it. Loved it. Beautiful.

Film Review

Pulp Fiction

Release: 1994, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

A super masc, violent set of random stories with no real moral to the story. I watched it at home on my own for my education but didn't enjoy it.

Film Review

The Mario Galaxy Movie

Release: 2026, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

As a Nintendo fan I was contractually obliged to watch this, but it really is mainstream nonsense. It's a series of happenings and references with no real meaning. I did get every reference though, which was satisfying.

Film Review

Pillion

Release: 2026, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

Wow, this is a steaming hot film about getting dommed by sexy biker man Alexander Skarsgård. It handles the topic excellently, is super funny, and I highly recommend it. Managed to get a free T-shirt from the Mongay preview!

Film Review

No Other Choice

Release: 2025, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

Had a solo trip to the cinema (Delphi Lux) while I was already feeling sad and upset and this just made it worse. It's an excellent film but wow, the plot really is upsetting for so many reasons.

Album Review

If My Wife New I'd Be Dead

Artist: CMAT, Release: 2022, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the albums page!

Having seen CMAT live and been absolutely blown away, I have been absolutely peaking on my CMAT obsession. This is her first album and it's full of the most real music. It's very country and western which is not normally something I'm into but hell yeah. It's all about 'I Wanna Be a Cowboy, Baby!' which sounds like it's from that vine (which it is), but it's actually a song about frustration with being a woman (among other things). It's great and I love it. She's also unafraid to get dark - 'Nashville' references this person who spent 8 months telling everyone they're moving away and after saying their goodbyes they killed themselves. Knowing that completely transforms the listening experience of that song for sure.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Splatoon 3

Platform: Switch, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

I don't like Splatoon music, I just don't think it's good. The weird voices, the not-very-good pop music. I hadn't even given this soundtrack much of a chance, but decided to return to it when they updated in 2026 with the DLC music.
It's still shit. It's all over the place. If it doesn't have the weird vocals, it's the most extreme, out-there noise music. If I were to listen to this on drugs I would have a meltdown. Just as an experiment, listen to 'Deluge Dirge' which is from the Salmon Run mode.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Super Mario Bros. Wonder

Platform: Switch 2, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

This game is really fun to play, but the music is ass. I had high hopes because I actually love the New Super Mario Bros music, but there's nothing good here really. I was excited by the trailer in the music because of the funny voice, but they only used it for like one track, such a disappointment.
In 2026 they added the Switch 2 version with Bellabel Park and honestly there's not much there. For a game so visually vibey, the music just doesn't reflect the vibes.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Super Metroid

Platform: SNES, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

This soundtrack is full of classics. It truly is the very best Metroid soundtrack and one of the best on the SNES. Personal favourites are the two Brinstar tracks. The first one is a groove and a bop, and the second is the ultimate unsettling chillout. Also shout-outs to the most insane boss music ever with Mother Brain. Wow.

Concert

CMAT

Date: 26-03-2026, Venue: Tempodrom, Berlin

This genuinely might have been one of the best concerts of my life. I went to see CMAT right at the height of my CMAT obsession, so it was a total pleasure to just bathe in her glory surrounded by all these well-dressed gays in the crowd! She is so funny, has such stage banter, would just stop songs to pose for a bit, she even spontaneously changed the setlist to accommodate requests from children in the audience. She did an interpretative dance of her experience not getting into Berghain! Also, a woman asked me out on a date at this concert. Highlight of the year for me.

Fun video for your entertainment

Weather lady glitch

Link: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ykz_dbpYRPU

I hope I see something like this live one day.

Fun video for your entertainment

Olivia Colman Oscar Speech

Link: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YUytvAg_hE4

I love her. Representing England.

Fun video for your entertainment

Love For Sale

Link: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SG4kUcS_DJs

A rare peep into a weird part of the Russian internet

New CD

Greatest Hits

Artist: Janis Joplin

Picked this up at the Boxi market after having lunch with Kitty

Smash character decoration in my house

Marco gave me this cute (official!) Nintendo merch.

And don't forget... an archive of "Soundtrack of the Day" updates are on the website!

item.title: Burnout from listening to too many smash remixes
item.description:
Well there haven't been any major updates on the website for like a month because I spent the whole time making an insane webpage which lets you listen to all the Smash Bros soundtracks. I was inspired by how much I've been enjoying listening to my own favourite songs on the daily soundtracks page, and by how I want to make my own music streaming situation. In the greater context of removing big tech from my life, how about that?
Well it was way more work than I anticipated! Smash Ultimate alone has over 1000 tracks and I sat down and listened to them all, because for whatever reason I took the opportunity to rate them all. I just figured, since I'm going through all the metadata anyway and making it perfect, and making little character pictures for each track, why not also just drop my opinions as well. Isn't that what this website is for?
Well I found myself blocking out hours of my free time to go through and form opinions on all of these franchises. For my favourites it was a total pleasure, but by the end I was really feeling like all those Castlevania remixes are blending into each other. And then just the insane amount of Fatal Fury, Tekken tracks for example, I was struggling. The real killer was the the original Smash tracks. Just repetitions of the same melody over and over. I was starting to go crazy. I started to think to myself, is this music all actually kinda shit? Like I love Nintendo music but I don't feel like this when I listen to normal music.
Well maybe it was all a bit much. And it's not like I have to do it again. Now, I can choose my favourites and just listen to those. And the work was still oddly satisfying so fuck it, I think it was worth it. I was able to play Slay the Spire 2 while working on it so that's cool. Slay the Spire 2 is gonna be game of the year by the way. Holy moly.
Well anyway my life has been trundling on since the last newsletter, nothing too much to report there. I hope some of you can enjoy the new smash soundtrack page, but if not, that's fine because I made it for me.

Game Review

Morsels

Vibes: 10, Gameplay: 8, Platform: Switch, Release: 2025

Squelching flesh-themed game where you play as disgusting little creatures battling other icky creations. They went all in on the weird organic matter aesthetic and it works! The music is great. We played it at Jack's while testing out new edibles.

Game Review

Minishoot' Adventures

Vibes: 7, Gameplay: 7, Platform: Xbox Gamepass, Release: 2024

Enjoyed trying this out on Fab's gamepass, it had some nice vibes and some very slick controls. We beat the first boss together but I don't think I will invest time and money into this.
Why on earth though does the name have an apostrophe in it.

Film Review

Marty Supreme

Release: 2025

Enjoyed returning to Kino International with Marf to watch this, although it's so intense from start to finish. I would be lying if I said I wasn't entertained, but god the main character is so insufferable. People like that make my skin crawl.

Film Review

Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon

Release: 2000

Got super stoned and watched this at home and loved it. Best martial arts ever. Excellent female characters.

Film Review

Iván & Hadoum

Release: 2026

I won tickets to see the Teddy Award winner at Mongay! Kitty and I went and it was lovely. A romantic story about a trans man in Andalucia. So great to see what it's like being a trans man, something I know not very much about.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Super Mario 3D World + Bowser's Fury

Platform: Switch, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

I do feel like the 3D World soundtrack is pretty mid, listening to it has reminded me of listening to the Captain Toad soundtrack some months ago for this website. But it is a bit more special than that at least. Mario Kart World's soundtrack introduced me to some nice tracks which has made me appreciate it more.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Kirby Air Riders

Platform: Switch 2, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

After two 'special releases' of this soundtrack, I am so delighted to see this first Switch 2 soundtrack make it onto the app! Now we have full unfettered access to the new generation of frenetic and maximalist Kirby music. Happy days! I will say I don't care for all these vocal songs that Sakurai likes to put in his games these days, but apart from that there is a lot to enjoy here. The highlight for me is lowkey the menu theme, which slaps so hard. Maybe it's because it reminds me of smash so much.
Otherwise this soundtrack continues the Kirby tradition of honouring past soundtracks with a wealth of returning music. A delight, as usual. Also, the entire original Air Ride soundtrack is here, and that music is pretty wild! Have a listen to 'Machine Passage' - is this Tekken?

Concert

Jens Lekman

Date: 08-03-2026, Venue: Gretchen, Berlin

I've always been a huge fan and finally I get to see him! He was touring his album about being a wedding singer, but the most delightful part was when he played a bunch of his old songs. Hearing 'Postcard to Nina' live in Berlin was everything.

Concert

Magdalena Bay

Date: 23-02-2026, Venue: Columbiahalle, Berlin

Went with Anni to see them for a third time! No regrets! Loved it. Was especially in the zone and especially delighted when they played some of their new songs.

New Photo in the Gallery

Imperialism is back baby

For anecdotes about the pictures, please visit the gallery page!

None

New CD

When I Said I Wanted To Be Your Dog

Artist: Jens Lekman

Picked this up at his concert. His oldest album. I love it.

New CD

I Know What Love Isn't

Artist: Jens Lekman

Picked this up at his concert. Special album with a special vibe.

New CD

Life Will See You Now

Artist: Jens Lekman

Picked this up at his concert. Wacky album that was recorded in Berlin.

And don't forget... an archive of "Soundtrack of the Day" updates are on the website!

item.title: New goal: remove big tech companies from my life
item.description:
Hey everyone and welcome to another newsletter. I've recently made a resolution for the second half of this decade, and that's to slowly but surely remove big tech from my life. First and foremost that means not adding any more big tech, like new subscriptions etc. From here on out, it's going to be less and less. I know that it's gonna be hard, I also can't imagine a life without Google Maps, but I'm gonna take it step by step.
Why go through the trouble? Because I'm acutely aware of the capabilities of the Palantir software that is being used all over the world. It can store data on people from all sorts of sources, and it's used for surveillance. It means that if the US does this thing where it demands to check your phone if you visit, my data will land in a centralised database that is used by ICE and stuff like that. When companies have your data, that data can be seized by authoritarian regimes! The world does appear to be going to shit, and who knows, maybe AfD will win big in Germany. I don't want super personal data like my location being saved anywhere.
So the goal is to use self-hosting. I've already learned a lot about it by making this website (hosted on a Raspberry Pi in my house!). Next step is to invest in a NAS (Network Attached Storage) and back up all my files and photos there instead of with Google. Further down the line I want to use OpenNAS to stream music too. I'm also interested in getting a dumbphone, and investing in a cute digital camera for taking photos. I've got lots of ideas! But I'm aware it will take a long time, so I'm giving myself 5 years to do it.
In other news, I've just gotten back from a beautiful trip to Tromsø, Norway with the family. It reminded me that travelling is awesome! I got on a plane and suddenly I was in a winter wonderland. I'm inspired to take many such trips this year as a solo traveller. I'd be lying if I said the trip was perfect though: Victor was meant to come and I really felt his absence. I'm excited to be back though - I have lots of fun plans for Fraser projects to attend to!
On the site there haven't been updates because I've been working on a webpage which lets you listen to the whole Smash Ultimate soundtrack. It's an insanely huge project which is taking lots of time (and I'm loving it).

Film Review

Wake Up Dead Man: A Knives Out Mystery

Release: 2025

Watched with this with the family but I wasn't blown away by it. Devout Christians are just too weird for me, like the parts where Josh O'Connor is being a 'good priest'. It was an appropriate film to watch with the family on holiday.

Film Review

Only Rebels Win

Release: 2026

Saw this at the opening of the Berlinale, there was a red carpet and stars wearing designer outfits and everything. The film was great. A reminder that it's great to not be a normie. Really loved it.

Film Review

Drunken Noodles

Release: 2025

I was a rolling my eyes a little at the supernatural aspects of this film. But it featured a great romantic moment between this guy and a man in his 70s.

Album Review

EURO-COUNTRY

Artist: CMAT, Release: 2025, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the albums page!

This lady is so fun and full of excellent vibes. She's loud and funny and I want to be her friend. The album is loosely about the 2008 financial crisis and the weird place it put Ireland in, and I love songs about politics. And she's got all these great songs! My absolute favourite is 'The Jamie Oliver Petrol Station', which is a meditation on hatred. She just hates Jamie Oliver and the song is about kicking herself for it because there's no point wasting time seething. It's such an anthem. Then there's 'Take a Sexy Picture of Me' which has the best chorus ever. It's got me singing along in my house 'I've been having a horrible time'! And the line about taking a picture to make her look 16? Oof! She's been there. And the song about the Tesla? Oof. Damn.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Pokémon FireRed Version and Pokémon LeafGreen Version

Platform: GBA, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

Happy days! I never believed they would do it! The app went this long without any classic Pokemon games and I was sure it was because of weird copyright holder reasons, but here we are - this classic OST finally has an official release! I really hope this signals the beginning of a new age of Pokemon and we get all the other generations on here, it would be a dream.
They remastered this! So many of these classic tracks sound even better than ever before. Just take a listen to the Trainer Battle music, which has cool panning and just sounds modern and nostalgic at the same time. Ah, I'm gushing. I really forgot how good that theme is, maybe because I've just heard it so much in my life.
I love the route themes, the battle themes, and I'm willing to admit it's the nostalgia speaking. But come on, it's gen 1. Gen 1 is everything. Rocket Hideout is right there.
Also!! Gen 2 is represented! We've got Azalea Town and a couple of others on the Sevii Islands music. Hell yes.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Paper Mario: The Origami King

Platform: Switch, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

What an absolute delight! Not only do they treat us with the Thousand Year Door, but also Origami King! This soundtrack is on the same level as TTYD for sure, those composers at Intelligent Systems know what they're doing. They always go above and beyond. In these games they're making pieces of music for every little thing. Sometimes you just hear these tracks just once for a few seconds! The result is an 11-hour soundtrack. By the end you might be fed up of this kind of music but not me, I think it's all quality.
Where to start? The incredible 10/10 battle music. Each area has its own variation of it, and they all fucking slap. The Red Streamer Battle is the original and I have to say the best, it's just so good. Yellow Streamer, Purple Streamer, Vellumental Battle and Autumn Mountain Battle are also favourites of mine. The battles themselves in the game were arguably the weakest part of the game (imagine how could this game could have been if it had Thousand Year Door battle mechanics...) but that didn't stop them having the best music ever.
In this game, the main antagonists are stationary items like scissors, rubber bands etc, which I find hilarious. When you fight the hole punch, you have to lure it out of its cave with some dance music, because it's actually a dancing hole punch (they animated that really well). You get a choice of four tracks and I remember playing the game trying the first three thinking 'these are all shit', then BAM, the fourth one is just an absolutely incredible disco banger! It's called 'Thrills at Night' and it's the only one to get the holepunch out. It's so fucking good I swear I'm going to DJ it.
There's also plenty of just great piano music, like the museum variation of Toad Town, or the excellent Coffee Break music. And 'Tranquil Pipes Teahouse' is literally just Maple Treeway! Wow!
Other highlights: Snif City! What a bop!

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Tomodachi Life

Platform: 3DS, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

At this point the Nintendo Music App is just a machine spewing out Mii games. There are just so many! They did just release a bunch of info about the new Tomodachi Life game but still, wow. I went into this with very low expectations (Tomodachi Collection before this was pretty shit), but actually there are some hidden bops here. 'Rankings Board: Girl Charm' is just Madonna's Vogue. The 'Precious Memories' trilogy has some really out-there vocals!
The listening experience as a whole is very mixed. You've got the huge selection of Compatibility Tester songs, first a bunch of piano songs, then the same music again but in band form, then both sets of music AGAIN but for romantic relationships.

New Photo in the Gallery

The North Pole

For anecdotes about the pictures, please visit the gallery page!

None

Smash character decoration in my house

A snippet from an old Nintendo Power Magazine

Smash character decoration in my house

I have always felt that Ness is creepy so I leaned into that when I realised I needed to draw two of them for the Power Ranking

Smash character decoration in my house

Bit of a weird tadpole-Mewtwo that I drew

Smash character decoration in my house

I drew Chrom as a duck

Smash character decoration in my house

A snippet from an old Nintendo Power Magazine

Smash character decoration in my house

The three pokemon as sardines

Smash character decoration in my house

Surprised myself by making an ultra-cute ROB-themed dragonfly

Smash character decoration in my house

It's meant to be Little Mac doing a pose on top of a pondskater

Smash character decoration in my house

I was inspired by Duck Hunt's swimming animation where the dog struggles and the duck just swims

Smash character decoration in my house

I drew the beautiful wings then made her a tiny butterfly and now it looks kinda weird

Smash character decoration in my house

Drew Joker as a fish for a power ranking

Smash character decoration in my house

Drew Joker as a fish for a power ranking

Smash character decoration in my house

Drew this steve who is drowning

Smash character decoration in my house

I drew Kazuya as a woodpecker

And don't forget... an archive of "Soundtrack of the Day" updates are on the website!

item.title: Moved by music
item.description:
As I listen to all my favourite songs, working hard on the 'daily soundtracks' part of this website, I am once again reminded that I am moved by music so easily and so regularly. I've already prepared song recommendations all the way up until July and it has been an utter delight. Whenever I remember a great song, I go down a rabbit hole and fill up the page with more and more of my favourite music. I've also realised that making the music player has been great for me, because it's a way I can listen to all my favourite tracks in a way that I would like. I have every intention of going crazy with this and letting hundreds of amazing songs and tracks fill up this raspberry pi, if nothing else for me, so that I can listen to it whenever I want.
I nice thing about this website is that it's largely separated from the wider internet (with the exception of Cloudflare - they do my DNS and security and it's simply not worth doing that myself). People are talking about doomsday scenarios where services like Spotify disappear and suddenly all the music files are gone. We will be fine, there are cool initiatives (check out Anna's Archive, recently sued by Spotify) who are scraping the company data for public use. But it just feels nice to be starting my own little collection of beautiful little music, which will always be there for me. And if the internet goes down, I will just host it raw on my raspberry pi for anyone who's in my apartment. It'll be like a CD collection.
This week, Victor broke up with me. It's been a real emotional rollercoaster. But I've listened to a lot of music, and feeling more sensitive than usual, have found myself ultra-connecting with it all. Any remotely sad song, even if it's completely unrelated to my life, I've been shedding tears. On the day of dumping it was 'To Be Loved' by Adele. The next day it was 'Lord Let That Tesla Crash' by my new obsession CMAT, which isn't even about love and relationships, but I still cried. Today I cried to 'The Linden Trees Are Still In Blosson' from Jens Lekman which is entirely unrelated but god. It hits.
My main coping mechanism for bad times is to be very productive and do many things, which is why I'm happy to say that I finished a text-based adventure that I've been working on since forever! I finally got round to writing all the various endings and trying to bring it all together. It was tough and I don't think I'm a good writer, but it's quite short, and it might be a fun 15 minute read for you! It's available on the 'miscellaneous' section of my website. If you spot some typos, please let me know. If anyone is interested in also making one, I used a program called 'Twine 2' which is great, I really recommend it.

Game Review

Hades 2

Vibes: 9, Gameplay: 9, Platform: Switch 2, Release: 2025

I'm enjoying the hypnotic loop for this game! It's hard to deny that Hades is one of the very best roguelikes out there, in a world saturated with roguelikes. It's polished, it's satisfying, and it really makes you want to keep playing.
The story and world-building is nice. I like that they made all the Greek gods and characters from mythology just insanely hot. I like that a frog can follow me around now, and it's cool that you can go up as well as down.
Part of me feels like I'm just going through the motions playing this. Hard to describe why.

Film Review

It Was Just an Accident

Release: 2025

Really great film with a horrible premise (it's about torture) but it's full of good people and a really thrilling story.

TV Show Review

Heated Rivalry

Release: 2025

This outrageous show about gay ice hockey players features so much hot gay sex between muscular men that it's practically porn. I totally understand why this show is so popular with women! What I loved about it was that there was no major trauma.

Film Review

Flash Gordon

Release: 1980

Terrible film but it was fun to laugh at it for an evening. There were some great costumes. America was America-ing hard on this one.

Album Review

Songs From The Big Chair

Artist: Tears For Fears, Release: 1985, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the albums page!

I've been loving this classic album from Tears for Fears! There are the famous songs like 'Shout' and 'Everybody Wants To Rule The World' and they're great but it's all about the album tracks. My favourite is 'The Working Hour' with its amazing saxophone. It's such a great vibe. You've also got the amazing 'Head Over Heels' which has such an amazing ending, followed by a very ambient and cerebral final track. It's such a great album for the end of the evening.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

WarioWare, Inc.: Minigame Mania

Platform: GBA, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

To think that they put WarioWare on here... amazing. Honestly as a soundtrack it's pretty unlistenable thanks to the vast majority of songs being just 4 seconds long. But there are some diamonds in the rough! The main title and menu themes are classics, thanks to smash, but the best song is 'Drifting Away' which is several minutes long and cycles through a bunch of different musical styles. It had compressed singing voices and it just sounds cute. It's honestly an all-time fave.
Also I didn't realise until listening to this just how much Wario Land 4 music is in there! I didn't know this game came out two years after that. It means we get a little of my all-time favourite Wario-themed track 'Crescent Moon Village' in the 'Elevator: Results' track. Result!

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

The Legend of Zelda: Phantom Hourglass

Platform: DS, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

The tinny DS soundfont is back to serve bass-less budget versions of Wind Waker music! I have to say even though I have fond memories of the game, the music really leaves a lot to be desired. The main offender is the dungeon music, a 30 second loop that is played for all 6 dungeons. It's terrible and an affront to Zelda music.
In an effort to be at least a little bit positive, Linebeck's theme slaps!

New Photo in the Gallery

Handling power tools

For anecdotes about the pictures, please visit the gallery page!

None

Smash character decoration in my house

Marta had these cool pens that you can use to decorate plates and now I have this gormless-looking Mii Gunner plate

And don't forget... an archive of "Soundtrack of the Day" updates are on the website!

item.title: It's pretty alright, pretty cold
item.description:
Berlin has been hit by a pretty real cold snap this month, and I've found myself super enjoying it. I'm out and about a lot, and I'm appreciating the -6 more than usual. I feel like in this age of climate change, mother nature will be telling us that she doesn't fuck around more and more often, and I'm kinda on her side. Luckily with my lifestyle I'm only ever really out in the biting cold wind for some moments before I can enter a warm building. Some of those feedback loops are going to start hitting soon when it comes to climate change, which I'm not very excited about. But Jack being so optimist when talking about the world's issues made me feel bad about dooming so much this week so maybe in 2026 we can all get excited about the prospect of nuclear fusion working. That would be sick.
I've thoroughly enjoyed this time around new years not just because of the cold though. Weyes put on not one but two great parties, and I actually had a bunch of time at home, so I finally got round to playing Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, which I loved. I can't wait to take some holidays from work exclusively to play games like this. That's where the priority is this year (sorry, Mexico).
Guys, I've spent a bunch of time on the daily soundtracks part of this website - you should check it out! There is an archive of all the previous daily soundtracks I've featured, and you can listen to them directly on the site, and even organise the tracks into little playlists. Doing this was very excited but now I'm suddenly aware that it's more important than ever that I only recommend the best songs ever. Luckily I'm overflowing with ideas about which music to recommend, and I've already made posts all the way into March. Anyway I hope it offers a fun reason to come back to the website from time to time.

Game Review

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33

Vibes: 10, Gameplay: 10, Platform: Playstation 5, Release: 2025

Wow! I have not played a game with such good art direction as this! Whoever they hired to design the outfits needs a raise. This game is so aggressively French and I'm so here for it. It's got berets, famous landmarks, and I just went ahead and played it in French for the full experience. Loved it. And the environments look so gorgeous and detailed and have amazing lighting and yeah this game just looks amazing.
And sounds amazing! Excellent, expansive soundtrack, unafraid to use accordion.
I think the best thing about the game though is the writing. It features characters who have actual empathy for one another! And when some crazy shit happens, they take a moment to talk about it. The characters are all distinct, flawed but likeable, and have their own reactions to everything. I love that Lune just wants to ask questions about everything because the story is so wild, she's just like me. And then Sciel is like the adult in the room, aware that the world around her is bigger than what she understands and decides to be chill about the whole thing. Maelle has a moment where she was like, this is not at all what I expected and I dunno I just thought it was so real, even if her scenes were a little hard to take seriously because I gave her a ridiculous haircut.
The gameplay is also good. It took me a long time to get into the parrying but towards the end of the game I started getting good at it. What's nice is that there are ways to play where you don't bother with the parrying at all. It meant I didn't feel like a total noob as I was starting the game. I will say that there is some optional content where you have to do platforming and it's totally bullshit. It was worth it to get the outfit of Verso just in his trunks though.

Game Review

Wario Land 3

Vibes: 6, Gameplay: 8, Platform: Gameboy, Release: 2000

Playing this game was so refreshing, it was familiar because I played and loved Wario Land 4, but also quite different to that game, and so different to your average platformer. In this game you can't die! It's more of a puzzle game - 'how do I get to this treasure chest?' rather than 'can I get to this treasure chest?'. I like all the transformations and the role they play in the puzzles. The game even has metroidvania elements the way you get new power-ups and revisit old levels.
The animations are also a lot of fun. It's such a goofy game. Delightful and recommended gem on the Nintendo Switch Online!

Game Review

Ball X Pit

Vibes: 6, Gameplay: 8, Platform: Switch 2, Release: 2025

This is so so so addicting, just like Vampire Survivors. Loved getting absolutely absorbed by it this Christmas. The ball part has some great risk/reward or push/pull and feels great. The city building part is a bit intimidating but actually alright in the end. I don't love the overall vibe and design of it, nor do I love the music. It's kinda gruesome and unpleasant. Honestly by the end I was happy I rolled the credits and I could stop.

Game Review

The Legend of Zelda: Four Swords

Vibes: 6, Gameplay: 7, Platform: Gameboy Advance, Release: 2002

Always wanted to play the multiplayer Zelda games and was finally able to when Oscar Andrew and I connected out Switch 2s on the NSO app and played local multiplayer. It was awesome and we blazed through the game in one sitting! It was consistently funny to bump into each other going through little corridors and hitting each other and we loved the whole thing. There were a couple of head-scratching moments and we weren't a fan of the mysterious Rupee Wraith. We just played it through once but it turns out that there's a true ending if you play it through on harder modes two more times. I feel like there's a lot of fun to be had here! The levels are even procedurally generated before that was cool. There's also a fun competitive element where the person who got the most rupees wins, Super Mario 3D World style. Loved that! Loved the game to be honest. Great.

Game Review

Super Mario Land 2: 6 Golden Coins

Vibes: 6, Gameplay: 6, Platform: Gameboy, Release: 1992

I enjoyed this game on the Nintendo Switch Online! Not as fun as other Mario games because of the small screen but glad to have played this one that I always missed. Cool that this is the first appearance of Wario!

Game Review

Metroid Prime 4: Beyond

Vibes: 9, Gameplay: 6, Platform: Switch 2, Release: 2025

This game started off so strong but by the end I decided that it isn't a true continuation of the excellent Metroid Prime series. Music and atmosphere is definitely great, although the only really memorable music is the first area, Fury Green. I feel like the game could benefit from more music tracks as actually most of the game is spent with just ambient noise.
The thing that I appreciate the most is that it takes place on a planet where the architecture is so yonic! Yonic is a fun word that means 'relating to vaginas and stuff' (as opposed to phallic). Every door, switch, building looks like female genitalia. Samus is going around controlling clits with her new psychic powers and you can't convince me otherwise. There is some pretty strong lesbian energy.
I also enjoy how so much random-ass shit happens in this game. When you beat one of the yonic bosses and then this pedestal appears which transports Samus to a dream realm where she grabs a magic key and then curls into a ball and learns how to boost up halfpipes. All that happens within like 2 minutes of each other.
Samus is a badass lone hunter who really doesn't need anyone else, but they introduce some annoying American characters who are stranded there anyway. I appreciate that they speak to Samus like she is a divine legend, and that Samus doesn't dignify them with any response, but ultimately they have been added as plot devices to try to add emotional weight to the story. It feels fake as fuck. It's just typical tropes like sacrificing yourself (and then turning up later), and has been totally shoehorned in. Would have been better if she was just on her own as usual. The best Metroid atmosphere is the feeling of loneliness. I don't want to get a cool new item and then immediately get a call from an American man saying where I should go next.
There's also a desert area where you drive around on a motorbike, a bit of a departure from the series, and it's fine, I guess. I actually appreciate how it makes it a bit more feasible to have these different climates (jungle, icy mountain, volcano) all in the same place. In Metroid Prime 1 you just hop between these biomes in a couple of seconds. That always bothered me!
Well, it would have been fine, except that at the end of the game, you have to drive around the desert and collect a bunch of these green crystals in order to progress to the end of the game. When it dawned on me that I only had half the crystals necessary, my heart sank. I then spent like an hour driving in this empty ass, shitty desert crashing into crystals over and over, just for the privilege of watching the ending? Bullshit. Hated it. Felt like work!
Then the ending, you don't even learn what Sylux’s whole deal is, unless you go for 100% (fuck that). I watched the secret cut scene on YouTube and well yeah that is a ridiculous backstory.
The gameplay itself was just the same as Metroid prime 1. I think they could have done more backtracking in a good way. The backtracking felt quite forced (American man tells you where to go), and how come we never return to the best place, Fury Green?
All in all, I'm pretty disappointed in this game and wouldn't recommend it. Play Metroid Prime 1 remastered instead!

Film Review

Carrie

Release: 1976

The first half of this classic film was unbearable to watch, but from the iconic moment at the school prom onwards we were just screaming 'Yeeees fuck em up!! Burn it all down!'. If only this is what happened when people treated women so badly in real life.

Film Review

The Great Escape

Release: 1963

The family has this on the TV at Christmas quite a lot. It was alright. Got me thinking if the Germans have some kind of equivalent.

Film Review

Aftersun

Release: 2022

Story of a dad taking his 11 daughter on holiday, trying to keep it together. I felt a real connection to him because he's my age. It all felt very real.

Film Review

Nickel Boys

Release: 2025

Very upsetting film about a segregated youth detention centre where horrible shit went down. POV camera the whole time makes it all feel like it's happening to you.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Mario & Luigi: Brothership

Platform: Switch, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

This soundtrack is definitely the biggest and most pleasant surprise of the app so far. I honestly thought it would be super generic music, similar to Princess Peach Showtime or something, but actually this music is gorgeous! You can tell from the soundtrack alone that the game was made with love, which is very important to me. The strength of these tracks is so good that I'm lowkey thinking about playing the game because of it, even if I was never a bit fan of the series (well I only ever tried Superstar Saga).
The battle theme is insanely good, and some of these tracks have stunning chord progressions (my weakness), like 'An Afternoon on an Islet' - what an incredible little tune. I was totally ready to dismiss this soundtrack but I've listened to the whole thing twice in one day and I have to say I love it.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Fire Emblem: Path of Radiance

Platform: Gamecube, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

I have always felt like this and Radiant Dawn's soundtrack sound a bit cheap and tinny thanks to their choice of orchestra sounds. Maybe it would slap more with a real orchestra but alas the game came out in 2005. Although all my actual favourites are on the Radiant Dawn soundtrack, there are still a whole bunch of great tracks here too. And even though a lot of the music sounds the same, there is some very special music here.
Some of the battle music is very lengthy and epic and multi-phased (thinking about the amazing 'Crimea Attacks' - which features in the Subspace Emissary!). Then you've got 'Against the Black Knight' which is so fucking good and perfect music for fighting an unbeatable foe. And there's this horrible character called Oliver who has his theme played on the harpsichord which is such a vibe. It's giving Castlevania and I love that.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Wii Party

Platform: Wii, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

We are treated to some more obscure Wii-era vibes with this game that probably nobody played. Deep breaths, there's no point being upset about it, I'm sure the actual good game soundtracks will all come in 2026. This one is a bit of a slog, similar to Mario Party, but there are some gems in there. Some of these tracks like 'Garden Gridlock' really evoke 2007 hard.

And don't forget... an archive of "Soundtrack of the Day" updates are on the website!

item.title: Shoutouts to 2025
item.description:
Merry Christmas! Welcome to the last newsletter of the year. I'm super happy to present my end of year list of video games. You can find it by going to the 'games' section and clicking '2025'. It's in order and yep, I put Hollow Knight Silksong at the top. What an amazing game!
I love lists, and ultimately that's why this website exists. It just feels so satisfying knowing I'm documenting everything in my life here. Did you know I also keep a daily diary? It's a little too personal for this website but I really recommend all readers to start one. It helps with the feeling of life running away from you.
It's been a particular pleasure to go to such lengths to document my life this year because this year is definitely the best of my life. It started with a super fun and life-changing trip to Mexico in January, then I continued working my excellent job which I really appreciate. I got a boyfriend! 2025 is also the year of the Nintendo Music App, which I have loved so much. It's something I've wanted since I was a child. And it's the year of this website! Working on this website has been a great learning experience and so so so fun. I'm so proud of it.
You guys have any new year's resolutions? Mine is to stop saying 'having time' and instead say 'making time'. I think saying 'I will try to make time for that' sounds more positive than 'I hope I have time for that' and will go far towards making me sound less like the calendar bitch that I actually am. My other resolution is to take more holidays from work when I feel like I have no time. There's no big Mexico month this year so I'll be focus on me a bit more this year.
See you guys next year!

Game Review

Metroid Prime 4: Beyond

Vibes: 10, Gameplay: 9, Platform: Switch 2, Release: 2025

This game is a true continuation of the excellent Metroid Prime series! Music and atmosphere is 100% there. As I write this I'm only half-way through the game so take this with a grain of salt for now, but it's great so far.
I really appreciate that it takes place on the planet where the architecture is so yonic! Yonic is a fun word that means 'relating to vaginas and stuff' (as opposed to phallic). Every door, switch, building looks like a vagina. Samus is going around controlling clits with her new psychic powers and you can't convince me otherwise. The game features some weak men who speak at you with American accents, but Samus doesn't even bother replying, which I really appreciate.
I also enjoy how so much random-ass shit happens in this game. When you beat one of the yonic bosses and then this pedestal appears which transports Samus to a dream realm where she grabs a magic key and then curls into a ball and learns how to boost up halfpipes. All that happens within like 2 minutes of each other.
There's also a desert area where you drive around on a motorbike, a bit of a departure from the series, and it's fine, I guess. I actually appreciate how it makes it a bit more feasible to have these different climates (jungle, icy mountain, volcano) all in the same place. In Metroid Prime 1 you just hop between these biomes in a couple of seconds. That always bothered me! Anyway, I'm excited to see how this game develops, for now I'm excited to keep playing over Christmas.

Game Review

Keeper

Vibes: 9, Gameplay: 4, Platform: Xbox Gamepass, Release: 2025

This game is like concept art come to life. It's got that Gris/Ori vibe of being beautiful for the sake of being beautiful. You play as a sentient lighthouse, which is a great premise, but ultimately it's another walking simulator with light puzzle elements. This one is different from other ones because it has amazing colours and psychedelic visuals. But ultimately, it's more of this same stuff. It makes me think that we really have enough indie games these days. Devs really have to stretch the imagination to think of new premises for new beautiful walking simulators these days.
I think it would be good to play this while on mushrooms.

Game Review

Kirby Air Riders

Vibes: 9, Gameplay: 8, Platform: Switch 2, Release: 2025

So delightful to play another Sakurai game! The smash vibes are very strong in this game. The soundtrack is pretty good, with an absolute banger of a menu theme, and the levels you race on are crazy cool. You can play as Rick the hamster, which is amazing, but I'm a little broken-hearted that you can't play as Kine the fish.
Controlling is kinda hard! This isn't baby mode like Mario Kart World (which I loved), you have to get a feel for it. Having both sucking in enemies and drifting being the same button is kinda confusing though. Also, there's an attack you can do if you wiggle the stick, which confuses me quite a lot. I really connected with the Road Trip mode, which in effect taught me the game. Thanks to that I tried out all the machines and got a hang of the drifting. I think my favourite is the Rocket one where you have to charge to the max each time. It's extreme but satisfying!

Film Review

The Secret Agent

Release: 2025

Vibes-heavy Brazilian thriller featuring (truly) the best female characters and the worst male characters. I struggled to keep up with the plot and the events towards the end felt a bit random.

Film Review

Bugonia

Release: 2025

Oh my god I loved this film so much. It was wild and hugely entertaining. Conspiracy theorist meets powerful CEO and the dialog between them is just perfect. Best ending ever

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Super Mario Land

Platform: Gameboy, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

This gameboy soundtrack actually has some classic bangers. It has inspired me to make a 'best bleeps and bloops' playlist. Birabuto is the classic but I am loving Muda Kingdom!

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Yoshi's Crafted World

Platform: Switch, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

Wow this soundtrack is tragically bad. They've gone for the little kid aesthetic to the max, recorders and all, and it honestly sounds terrible. Lowlights include 'Yoshi's Expedition' and 'What's It Gonna Be?', both of which sound like infants trashing a music schoolroom.
The soundtrack has two saving graces! The uncharacteristic 'A Teeny, Tiny Universe' which is good because it sounds like it came from another game, and the very last track 'Round and Round' which I guess is meant to be a joke but it low-key a bop.

Nintendo Soundtrack Review

Metroid Prime 4: Beyond

Platform: Switch, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

Wow these tracks are super long. I love a lengthy loop, especially when you know that you're gonna be hanging out in a place for a long time. The vibes appear to be there, so I'm excited! At the time of writing, I've just started the game and experienced the majesty of 'Fury Green' in game. I think it's gonna be a banger.

New Photo in the Gallery

Visit to Müritz

For anecdotes about the pictures, please visit the gallery page!

None

New Photo in the Gallery

Smash Tournament in Frankfurt

For anecdotes about the pictures, please visit the gallery page!

None

New CD

Shoot Your Shot Anthology

Artist: Divine

Victor got me this amazing CD of cunty pop tracks from the legendary Divine! Perfect for pre-drinks for the party

And don't forget... an archive of "Soundtrack of the Day" updates are on the website!

item.title: Entitled to Nintendo Music
item.description:
Hey everyone, it's me again. Quick update about the website: I added some TV shows to the Films section. I also made a tierlist of every pokemon! It was a lot of fun and it took me a whole morning. I'm thinking about slowly ranking the pokemon within the tiers later, but we'll see. Interestingly, for that page I added over 1000 images to the website and it hit me that this website in total has around 3000 images, and a bunch of mp3s on the music page. I then spent an entire afternoon and evening reorganising my images and assets and now the website is lightweight again! Even myself I feel a little spring in my step, like a burden has been lifted from my shoulders. Anyway, on to today's main topic...
As regular visitors of this site might know, my year has been drastically improved by the appearance of the new Nintendo Music App. One of the biggest parts of this site is my weekly reviews of each new release. I love it so much, I've even started observing a strange kind of mysticism in which the kind of soundtrack released on a Tuesday dictates what kind of week I'm going to have. As somebody who doesn't tolerate astrology I know that it's bullshit but I can't help but think about it whenever I am delighted or disappointed by a particular release.
It's a very niche interest. Not many people I know love Nintendo music to the extent that I do. It means it's a struggle to find people to be enthusiastic about the release on Tuesday with. That's why I'm eternally grateful to my two mates from home, Oscar and Andrew, who are actually willing to discuss and rate each soundtrack when it comes! We have a little Whatsapp group for it and I honestly love it so much. As I write this, I'm listening to the Wii Fit soundtrack, which is full of surprising bangers, thinking that my week has the potential to be 'surprisingly good' and in general appreciating this era of Nintendo music vibes. It's so good.
Recently I found myself wondering if anyone else out there is living their lives week to week like me, just waiting for the next release. Surely there are others who have spend the last decades lamenting the lack of official releases of these classic soundtracks? Surely other people are as excited as me by having a full on feature-rich app with a bunch of these OSTs on them, having them drip-fed to us? So I went onto reddit.com.
I've been on reddit.com a couple of times and I almost always have a bad time. I get that it has real people on there, one of the few places left that does. But everytime I visit I'm reminded how they're all Americans there, and they're all Negative Nancies. They just complain! I found a subreddit r/nintendomusic where there is a bunch of spam, and then each week a thread about the week's release in the app. Excited I clicked on Wii Fit's release and all I see are a bunch of sarky comments about they don't get a real soundtrack like Mario or Zelda, how shit the app is, etc. etc.
This bothers me so much because a) the app comes free with the already insanely cheap Nintendo Switch Online subscription. They were paying for this thing already and then bam, free music app to go with it. Then with each release, they go onto reddit.com and complain! They feel entitled to more. Why not release the app with all the soundtracks on it already? How dare they release Wii Fit when Majora's Mask isn't even on there yet? These keyboard warriors need to calm down, do some deep breathing and just enjoy the Wii Fit soundtrack.
I mean I too have been disappointed by the initial catalog and certain releases (looking at you OOT 3D and Metroid 2) but I've learned to appreciate what I'm given. Occasionally there is an amazing release (Paper Mario, TOTK) which catapults me into two weeks of reliving those games and I love it so much. If they gave us everything from the beginning for free, I don't think I would be as hooked on the app as I am. Also, I have to say that as an app developer myself I get it. Making features takes ages and writing content takes ages. These soundtracks all have screenshots as artwork, playlists associated with each track, and all the work behind getting the soundtracks themselves. It's a bunch of work and I get it.
Maybe people feel entitled to this music because it's all available on Youtube for free already. That's fair enough, and to be fair, only on this one subreddit I actually found any discussion on it. I think most people completely ignore this app. Anyway, at least there's one website covering these important releases!
Here's hoping that it continues, becoming an exhaustive bank of great music over the coming years. I'll be there every step of the way. Love live the Nintendo Music App!

Game Review

CloverPit

Vibes: 9, Gameplay: 8, Platform: Xbox Gamepass, Release: 2025

A cool vibe from a hellish gambling game where it's not actually about gambling, it's about optimising and finding bottlenecks and scaling numbers. It's actually just Balatro. It's Balatro all over again with a slot machine instead of poker hands. Fab and I got a really good run on our first run because we got some great synergies with diamonds. I'm interested to keep playing to know if it's possible to leave the room but something tells me it's not. Cool game, just would have been better if it were released BEFORE Balatro...

Game Review

Kirby's Dream Land 2

Vibes: 7, Gameplay: 5, Platform: Gameboy, Release: 1992

This is the kirby game with the 3 animal friends. You spend most of the game riding them and honestly I think it detracts from the experience a bit.

Game Review

Survival Kids

Vibes: 7, Gameplay: 4, Platform: Gameboy, Release: 1999

I saw that they realised a newer Survival Kids game, but only on the Nintendo Switch Online did I see that it's a long series with a Gameboy release in 1999! Turns out, this game might be the first ever survival-type game. Probably the reason why games like 'Don't Starve' exist. So for science I booted it up and gave it a good try.
First, I was pleasantly surprised by the banger music. Always a great sign when the OST ist good. I was looking for materials while grooving to the max. It's almost as if the child that I'm playing as hasn't been stranded alone on an island! Considering it's a game about not dying, it's surprisingly peppy and fun. The intro story was funny, it said the child and their dad were the only crew on the boat when it was shipwrecked, and then it shows just a massive cruise ship sinking. No wonder it sank - there were only two people on board!
I didn't make it far in the game, regrettably. It hit me that there's a reason that I didn't enjoy 'Don't Starve'. At any moment, my hunger or thirst could drop to zero and then bam, I have to start the whole game again! That is so stressful. And the game really doesn't do any signposting at all. I don't think it's possible to actually complete it without some kind of guide.

Game Review

Kirby's Dream Land

Vibes: 8, Gameplay: 6, Platform: Gameboy, Release: 1992

I managed to beat this entire game while Victor took the dog out! It was delightful, although actually a little harder than your usual Kirby game because there were no broken copy abilities. Great to see the origin of so much Kirby stuff. Loved the King Dedede fight in which he used dash attack, up special, f-smash, neutral special. Great. Music is also 10/10.

Game Review

Ristar

Vibes: 8, Gameplay: 6, Platform: Sega Mega Drive, Release: 1995

I had vaguely heard of this game so I gave it a go on the Nintendo Switch Online and was delighted by the amazing soundtrack. The game itself was quite forgiving for a mega drive game! Although as a platformer it's nothing special. I'm grateful that I now know about its great OST though.

Game Review

Ecco the Dolphin

Vibes: 9, Gameplay: 4, Platform: Sega Mega Drive, Release: 1992

I've been aware of the soundtracks of these games, made by Tim Follin, one of my favourite composers, for a long time. They are baffling, expansive and vibe-heavy games in which you play as a dolphin! Truly a first in the dolphin genre. I also heard that they are hard as balls so I was curious, and indeed, it's hard as balls. It's totally confounding and has no information about where to go or what to do. You just swim around and die to random things all the time. Definitely the kind of game to just listen to the soundtrack to and not actually play.

Game Review

Streets of Rage 2

Vibes: 10, Gameplay: 9, Platform: Sega Mega Drive, Release: 1992

Ever since falling in love with Streets of Rage 4, I've been meaning to get into this game as well, and I'm so glad I did! The reason to open up this game on the Nintendo Switch Online is so you can listen to one of the best soundtracks of all time. It's inspired by house and techno music and each track is a fucking banger. I have literally DJ'd some of these tracks before (Go Straight and Dreamer) they are that good. They hold up today!
The opening music is the biggest vibe ever, although booting up the game I realised you can't actually listen to the whole opening track before the opening ends. Devastating.
